'''Josh Simm''' (born 27 February 2001) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]]. He is the grandson of [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] legend [[Geoff Pimblett]].

He has previously played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] and [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[Wynnum Manly Seagulls]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]. He has spent time on loan at [[Leigh Centurions]] in both the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the Super League, and at [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] in the Super League.

==Background==
Simm was born in [[St Helens, Merseyside|St Helens]], England. He is the grandson of [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] legend [[Geoff Pimblett]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.saintsrlfc.com/2018/09/27/trio-promoted-to-2019-squad/|title=Callum Hazzard, Jack Welsby and Josh Simm will be promoted to the first team in 2019|date=27 September 2018 |publisher=St Helens RLFC}}</ref>

==Playing career==

=== St Helens ===
In the 2019 under 19s academy season, Simm scored a total of 30 tries in 20 games, beating his previous season's total of 29 tries in 23 games.{{fact|date=January 2024}}

Simm made his [[Super League]] début for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] against the [[London Broncos]] in July 2019.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP2921798|title=Super League: London Broncos v St Helens|publisher=BBC}}
</ref>

Simm would go on to score his first Super League try in round 17 of the [[Super League XXV results|2020 Super League season]] vs [[Leeds Rhinos]], where he scored a hat-trick in a man of the match performance.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-leeds/report/54307|title=St Helens 40-8 Leeds |publisher=Sky Sports |date=23 October 2020}}</ref>

Simm made seven appearances for the [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in 2022 including the Challenge Cup semi-final defeat to [[Wigan Warriors]]. The outside back also made a further five Super League appearances for [[Hull FC]] where he spent the second half of last season on loan, scoring two tries.{{fact|date=January 2024}}

==== Leigh Centurions (loan) ====
On 13 April 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the [[Super League]] on a short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=13 Apr 2021|accessdate=13 Apr 2021|title=Leigh sign Josh Simm on loan from St Helens|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-sign-josh-simm-on-loan-from-st-helens/}}</ref>

=== Wynnum Manly Seagulls ===
In October, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] announced Simm would be moving on elsewhere. On 24 October, Simm signed for the [[Wynnum Manly Seagulls]] in the [[Queensland Cup]] in Australia for the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/exclusive-former-st-helens-outside-back-josh-simm-signs-for-brisbane-broncos-feeder-club-wynnum-manly-seagulls-60974/|title=Former St Helens outside back Josh Simm signs for Brisbane Broncos' feeder club Wynnum Manly Seagulls |publisher=Serious About Rugby League |date=24 October 2022}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 3 October 2023, the [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Simm on a two-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=3 October 2023 |title=Simm joins Castleford |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8782 |access-date=7 October 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=3 October 2023 |title=Josh Simm: Castleford Tigers sign ex-St Helens centre for 2024 season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67000446 |access-date=7 October 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He said, "I'm absolutely buzzing, I really can't wait! The Cas fans can expect full commitment, and 100% effort in every game, and I pride myself in being able to score from anywhere in attack."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=3 October 2023 |title=Josh Simm out to silence doubters after landing Castleford Tigers deal |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/josh-simm-castleford-tigers-deal-27835560 |access-date=7 October 2023 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref>

Simm was assigned squad number 2 ahead of the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=20 January 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers 2024 squad numbers: 11 new signings receive their jerseys, with 31 shirts handed out |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-2024-squad-numbers-11-new-signings-receive-their-jerseys-with-31-shirts-handed-out |access-date=2 March 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He made his Castleford debut in round 1 against the [[Wigan Warriors]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Freeman |first=Jay |date=17 February 2024 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 4-32 Wigan Warriors - Reigning champions begin with hard-earned win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304778 |access-date=2 March 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> and scored his first try in round 3 against the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=1 March 2024 |title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 30-8 Castleford Tigers - Comfortable win for Wire |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68438389 |access-date=2 March 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In round 5, he scored two tries against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=16 March 2024 |title=Super League: Catalans Dragons 40-14 Castleford Tigers - Fourth win in five games for French side |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68569765 |access-date=17 March 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

In the first game of the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025 season]], Simm made his first appearance in 10 months and scored a length-of-the-field try against [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[2025 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=9 February 2025 |title=Bradford upset Cas as Salford & Giants go through |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce8jm7y7m34o |access-date=14 February 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He finished the year as Castleford's top tryscorer with 8 in 21 appearances.<ref name="RLRKC" />

===Catalans Dragons===
On 6 September 2025, it was announced that Simm would join [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] from 2026 on a two-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=6 September 2025 |title=Off-contract Castleford Tigers star seals cross-Super League switch for 2026 |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/off-contract-castleford-tigers-star-seals-cross-super-league-switch-for-2026 |access-date=28 September 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

He is currently appearing for the [[Catalans Dragons]] reserve side, [[Saint-Estève XIII Catalan]], in the [[Super XIII]].
